detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Howard Schultzâs leadership style influenced the organizational structure and culture of Starbucks Co.?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear, wellâfocused research question consistently tied to discussion
Comprehensive explanation of topic with detailed context and motivation
Methodology uses a wide range of relevant secondary sources with clear justification
weaknesses: Overâreliance on Starbucksâ own PR materials in places
Minor repetition and occasional digressions in the narrative
No primary data to triangulate findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments consistently placed Focus and Method in the 5â6 band. The student communicates topic and research question accurately and effectively, and the methodological justification is strong. Minor narrative repetition and the absence of primary work prevent a full 6.

strengths: Effective and largely coherent use of business theories (Bass, Mintzberg, Schein)
Clear and consistent application of relevant source material to support arguments
Terminology is used accurately and consistently throughout
weaknesses: A few long descriptive passages lack critical linkage to the argument
Occasional promotional citations without sufficient critical framing
Minor stylistic inconsistencies in presentation of figures and tables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear, coherent subject knowledge and mostly relevant application of sources, meriting the top of the 3â4 band or bottom of 5â6 band. Two strands achieved bandâ5 performance; one was in bandâ4. A score of 5 reflects strong mastery with minor areas for deeper synthesis.

strengths: Extensive, current research clearly aligned to the research question, including academic journals, market data, and corporate reports
Sophisticated analysis with wellâsupported conclusions, e.g. linking idealized influence to empirical figures
Reasoned argument structure with meaningful evaluation of limitations and suggestions for further study
weaknesses: Some analytical threads revert to narrative description rather than critical contrast
Counterâarguments and alternative explanations are acknowledged but not deeply explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although strand scores were placed in the 7â9 band, the overall critical depth, breadth of sources and nearâcomprehensive evaluation justify placement at the top of the 10â12 band. Only very minor gaps (primary data, brief narrative default) prevent a perfect 12.

strengths: Wellâstructured layout with clear headings, captioned figures and tables
Consistent application of Harvardâstyle citations and inclusion of word count/title
Logical flow through introduction, methodology, analysis and conclusion
weaknesses: Page numbers are not visible in the excerpt, hindering navigation
Long URLs and uneven table cell formatting slightly disrupt visual consistency
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all required structural and formatting conventions, placing it firmly in the top band. Minor lapses in page numbering and layout details limit the criterion to 4 rather than a perfect but nonâexistent higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicit (âTo what extent has Howard Schultz's leadership style influencedâ¦â) and is consistently referred to throughout analysis and conclusion. Scope is neither too broad nor too narrow, allowing sustained focus on two organisational dimensions. Occasional overlap between structure and culture sections shows slight dilution of focus, hence 5 rather than 6.

A clear line of reasoning is sustained and limitations are acknowledged in the conclusion; the student proposes further STEEPLE analysis and notes single-style bias. Evaluation is present but not fully criticalâalternative leadership explanations or counter-evidence are limited. Hence quality aligns with lower 7-9 marks.

The essay demonstrates clear, coherent grasp of transformational leadership, matrix structures and culture theory. Business concepts are explained accurately and applied to Starbucks. Depth across two functional areas shows effective understanding; however, occasional textbook regurgitation without synthesis prevents a perfect 6.

Terminology such as âindividualised considerationâ, âprofessional bureaucracyâ, âartifactsâ, âmatrixâ is used correctly and consistently. Definitions are provided and later applied. Minor spelling slips (âquidingâ) and a few stylistic inconsistencies limit the flawless consistency required for 6, so 5 is awarded.

The student moves beyond description, applying Bass, Mintzberg and Schein to dissect causal links. Arguments are logical and mostly well-supported. Nonetheless, analytical depth sometimes plateaus into narrative (e.g., extensive listing of regional splits without weighing alternatives), so it meets but does not exceed the 7-9 levelâs baseline.

Source material is generally well-integrated; quotes, statistics and corporate documents directly support arguments. Nevertheless, several long descriptive passages paraphrase without critical linkage (e.g., extended benefits lists) and a few citations are promotional. Relevance is thus âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ, fitting the 3-4 band top mark.

A wide range of current academic, corporate and media sources is listed; selection is justified in the methodology, and business frameworks (Bass, Mintzberg, Schein) are purposefully chosen. Evidence of primary work is absent, yet for a secondary-research EE the sourcing is appropriate and clearly informed. Minor weaknesses (heavy reliance on Starbucksâ own PR site) keep it at 5.

Research incorporates academic journals, books, corporate reports and market data, all clearly linked to the question. Breadth and currency are strong; triangulation is attempted (e.g., independent criticism of sustainability claims). A lack of primary stakeholder data and some reliance on company publications restrain it to the lower end of the 7-9 band.

Formatting elements (word count, title, Harvard-style citations, figure labelling) are present; tables and images are captioned. Page numbers are implied by TOC but not visible in excerpt, and a few long URLs break layout. These minor lapses justify 3 rather than 4 within the highest band.

The student clearly communicates a focused topicâthe influence of Schultzâs leadership on Starbucksâ structure and cultureâearly in the title, abstract sentence and introduction. Background context, personal motivation and relevance of the study are explained in detail, showing comprehensive clarity. Minor repetition and some narrative digressions prevent the âoutstandingâ feel, so a solid 5 (upper-band) is appropriate rather than the full 6.

The essay follows EE requirements: title page, TOC, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, works cited. Logical flow within sections is strong with subsection headings aiding navigation. Therefore it attains the top band for structure.

annotations.comment: Figure 2 is well-placed and captioned, visually reinforcing the theoretical model.
The title precisely communicates the investigationâs focus on leadership influence, setting a clear scope from the outset.
Page numbers are omitted in the TOC, which may hinder quick reference and distract from clear presentation.
Introduction provides rich context and personal motivation, enhancing engagement and demonstrating clear focus.
Consider incorporating primary interviews or surveys to triangulate findings and add original insight.
The tools and theories section ends mid-sentence, which interrupts clarity; complete the description for coherence.
Terminology is used accurately and coherently with appropriate citations, demonstrating strong subject knowledge.
The research question is clearly stated and well-focused, providing a strong anchor for the analysis throughout the essay.
Word count is provided prominently, meeting the formatting requirement and confirming compliance with length limits.
The table of contents is clearly structured with logical headings, aiding navigation and coherence.
The personal anecdote is somewhat informal for an academic EE; consider adjusting tone to maintain scholarly voice.
Methodology clearly outlines secondary source selection with justification, demonstrating informed and relevant choice.
Minor spelling error (âquidingâ instead of âguidingâ) detracts from professionalism; correct for presentation.
Link the theoretical definition explicitly to why leadership style impacts organizational structure and culture.
Detailed examples of idealized influence effectively illustrate deep analysis and application of theory.
Consider converting lengthy list items into narrative paragraphs to improve flow and integration.
The quantified figure of $33 million reinforces the argumentâs credibility with empirical support.
Video citations (e.g., Summit clips) lack full source details; include complete reference information.
Effective use of Scheinâs model provides a solid framework for discussing organizational culture.
Long paragraphs reduce readability; break into shorter units to maintain reader engagement.
Table cell padding is inconsistent, affecting visual uniformity; adjust cell formatting for consistency.
Methodological limitations beyond theoretical lens could be expandedâconsider discussing data source biases.
The term âdynamic environmentâ is vague; specify which external factors influenced the structure and culture.
The works cited list demonstrates a breadth of academic and media sources, reflecting thorough research.
Some entries lack consistent italics and ordering of elementsâstandardize formatting to Harvard style.
Citation split across lines (URL and details) disrupts flow; keep entries intact for readability.
Use of recent 2024 sources (e.g., Indeed article) demonstrates currency and relevance in research.
Footer number intruding into citation area disrupts layout consistency; reposition for clarity.
Ethical sourcing citation enriches the discussion of espoused values by linking to concrete CSR initiatives.
Several sources lack access dates (e.g., Schein book) which are important for online references; add retrieval details.
After presenting Figure 2, integrate specific references to its components in the narrative for stronger linkage.
Mintzbergâs table is comprehensive and captioned correctly, aiding conceptual clarity.
The conclusion succinctly summarizes findings, restating how Schultzâs leadership shaped structure and culture.
